denial_letter_text:
Dear Member,

Cigna has completed its review of the prior authorization request submitted on your behalf and is unable to approve the requested service.

Continuation of the requested service requires an active prior authorization on file. The prior authorization that previously applied has lapsed, and our records do not reflect a current, approved authorization. A new prior authorization request must be submitted and approved before the requested service will be covered under your plan.

This determination applies the plan's standard authorization requirements to all requests of this type and does not reflect any review of the member's diagnosis, the treating provider, or the individual clinical records associated with this request.

A peer-to-peer review may be available regarding this determination. The timeframe for requesting a peer-to-peer discussion was communicated verbally to your provider's office by phone only and is not confirmed in this letter.

If you disagree with this determination, you may file an internal appeal by contacting the number on your member identification card, and you may request an external review by an independent review organization once your internal appeal rights are exhausted.

This letter addresses coverage under your plan only and is not medical advice.

Sincerely,
Cigna Utilization Management
clinical_context: The patient is a 21-year-old man with B-cell acute lymphoblastic leukemia (C91.00) in the maintenance phase of standard-of-care chemotherapy. He achieved a complete morphologic and measurable-residual-disease-negative remission after induction and consolidation, and is now on oral maintenance therapy — daily 6-mercaptopurine and weekly methotrexate with monthly vincristine and steroid pulses — planned to continue for a total of about two to three years. The prior authorization covering his maintenance regimen lapsed at the plan's renewal cycle; there has been no change in his diagnosis, regimen, or treatment need, and interrupting maintenance therapy in the first years of remission carries a real risk of relapse. His oncologist's office was told a peer-to-peer review window by phone but received nothing in writing confirming the deadline, so the office cannot verify or meet it. The complete treatment records — the remission documentation, the regimen, and the maintenance schedule — were submitted with the re-authorization request, none of which the denial notice addresses.

matrix_coverage.reasoning: matrix_cell matches field-for-field; C91.00 B-cell ALL is oncology; maintenance-chemo continuation realistic; rationale instantiates continuation_of_care_lapsed; age 21 in 18-25 band.

scenario_realism.reasoning: 21M B-ALL maintenance on 6-MP/methotrexate with a lapsed re-auth at renewal is textbook; pure-boilerplate denial and a verbally-only peer-to-peer window are authentic exploitable defects.

insurer_voice.reasoning: Branded Cigna with procedural cadence and no wrong-insurer tells, but administrative lapse denial cites no Cigna-specific MCG/Medical Necessity Guidelines.
insurer_voice.improvement: Reference Cigna MCG / Medical Necessity Guidelines.

denial_logic.reasoning: Authentically shoddy continuation_of_care_lapsed: denies on absent active authorization, disclaims clinical review, no re-auth deadline or threshold; verbal-only P2P leaves an exploitable gap; appealable, not hallucinated.

clinical_realism.reasoning: Standard ALL maintenance backbone (daily 6-MP, weekly methotrexate, monthly vincristine/steroid pulses) over 2-3 years; MRD-negative remission terminology correct; relapse risk on interruption sound. No biology violations.

diagnosis_treatment_match.reasoning: Oral 6-MP plus methotrexate is the standard maintenance regimen for B-cell ALL; clean match.

diversity_delta.reasoning: Only Cigna and only continuation_of_care_lapsed case; ALL maintenance / oral antimetabolite trajectory distinct from neighbour DLBCL R-CHOP and CLL targeted-therapy cases.

diversity_statistical.reasoning: Max trigram-Jaccard: corpus=0.148 (nearest=case_119_cigna_priorauth), neighbours=0.0. Thresholds: warn>=0.35, fail>=0.5.

safety_redactor.reasoning: No banned topics; curative oncology in remission (not end-of-life); no SI; adult 21M; Cigna commercial, no government-payer framing.

contradiction_hunter.reasoning: Gender, diagnosis, regimen, lapse and verbal-P2P facts stable across documents; 'no clinical review' vs submitted records is the appealable falsehood, not an unstable fact; internally consistent.

llm_tell_detector.reasoning: Formal insurer boilerplate and dense chart-style prose; no GPT vocabulary, listicles, or meta-commentary.

overall_tone.reasoning: Cold procedural Cigna register with administrative obfuscation; clinical context plain factual narrative, relapse risk stated neutrally.

financial_auditor.reasoning: Silent on cost liability (standard practice); no dollar figures; no Medicare/Medicaid conflation.

legal_auditor.reasoning: Generic internal/external review rights, no hallucinated statutes, no Medicare framing; verbal-only P2P window and absent ERISA specifics are realistic attackable imperfections.

demographic_validator.reasoning: 21M with B-cell ALL in the AYA band is epidemiologically sound (slight male predominance); consistent male pronouns; no stereotyping.

scope_guard.reasoning: Cigna in-scope, self-funded commercial ERISA, adult 21, member-facing appeal rights only, no live-filing/representation language.

date_sanity.reasoning: No explicit calendar dates; relative durations ('two to three years', 'renewal cycle') coherent with no contradictions, but no anchored 2025-2026 dates.
date_sanity.improvement: Anchor a request/denial date and the appeal window.

citation_traceability.reasoning: Only generic 'standard authorization requirements' references, no hallucinated alphanumeric codes; realistically vague.

appeal_difficulty.reasoning: Denial reviewed nothing and ignored submitted records for a guideline-standard in-remission maintenance regimen; verbal-only unverifiable P2P deadline; administrative lapse easily cured. Easy multi-vector appeal.
exploitable_weaknesses: Denial explicitly conducted no clinical review and ignored the complete submitted records, so it cannot defend medical necessity for a guideline-standard, in-remission maintenance regimen
Peer-to-peer deadline communicated only verbally by phone and not confirmed in writing, making the window unverifiable and unmeetable — a procedural/due-process defect
Administrative lapse at renewal with no change in diagnosis, regimen, or need; interrupting maintenance risks relapse
strong_defenses: The plan can correctly assert that its standard authorization requirements apply to all requests of this type and that the prior authorization had in fact lapsed, so a current approved authorization is a legitimate coverage prerequisite
